chapters.title: て-form & Connecting Actions
chapters.description: Use the te-form to connect verbs, make requests, and describe sequences.

lessons.prompt: What is the て-form (te-form) of 食べる (taberu)?
lessons.explanation: 'Tabete' is the te-form of taberu. For ichidan (ru) verbs: remove ru, add te. 食べる→食べて.

lessons.prompt: ちょっと待っ___ください。(Please wait a moment.)
lessons.explanation: 'Matte kudasai' = please wait. Te-form + kudasai = polite request. 待つ (matsu) → 待って (matte).

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'I eat and then go to school'
lessons.correctAnswer: 食べて学校へ行きます
lessons.hint: Te-form connects sequential actions: tabete (eating, then...) gakkō e ikimasu.

lessons.prompt: What does 'て-form + います' express?
lessons.explanation: Te-form + imasu = progressive or habitual. '食べています' = is eating / eats (habitually).

lessons.prompt: 日本語を勉強し___います。(I am studying Japanese.)
lessons.explanation: 'Benkyou shite imasu' = I am studying. Suru verb: benkyou suru → benkyou shite (te-form).

chapters.title: Potential & Desire Forms
chapters.description: Express ability, possibility, and wants using potential and たい forms.

lessons.prompt: What does '日本語が話せます' (Nihongo ga hanasemasu) mean?
lessons.explanation: Potential form expresses ability. 話す (hanasu) → 話せる (hanaseru) = can speak. が marks ability object.

lessons.prompt: すしが食べ___です。(I want to eat sushi.)
lessons.explanation: 'Tai' form = want to do. Remove masu from polite form, add tai: 食べます→食べたい. Conjugates like an i-adjective.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'I cannot read kanji'
lessons.hint: Potential form of 読む (yomu) = 読める (yomeru). Negative polite: 読めません.

lessons.prompt: How do you say 'I wanted to go' (past desire)?
lessons.explanation: 'Tai' is an i-adjective. Past = 'takatta': 行きたかった = wanted to go. 行きたい = want to go.

lessons.prompt: 日本に___たいです。(I want to go to Japan.)
lessons.explanation: 'Ikitai desu' = I want to go. Remove 'masu' from 'ikimasu' (行きます) → 行き + たい.

chapters.title: Giving & Receiving
chapters.description: Master the complex Japanese system of giving and receiving verbs.

lessons.prompt: When do you use あげる (ageru)?
lessons.explanation: 'Ageru' = to give (from my side to others). 'Kureru' = to give (from others to me). 'Morau' = to receive.

lessons.prompt: 友達がプレゼントを___れました。(My friend gave me a present.)
lessons.explanation: 'Kuremashita' = gave (to me). Use 'kureru/kuremasu' when someone gives something to you (the speaker).

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'I received a book from my teacher'
lessons.correctAnswer: 先生に本をもらいました
lessons.hint: 'Moraimashita' = received. 'Ni' marks the source. 先生に = from the teacher.

lessons.prompt: What does 'te-form + あげる' express?
options: Doing a favor for someone else
lessons.correctAnswer: Doing a favor for someone else
lessons.explanation: 'Te-form + ageru' = do something for someone. '教えてあげる' = I'll teach (as a favor to them).

lessons.prompt: 先生が日本語を教えて___ました。(The teacher taught me Japanese — as a favor to me.)
lessons.explanation: 'Oshiete kuremashita' = teacher taught me (doing me a favor). Te-form + kureru = someone does something for me.

chapters.title: Conditionals — と, ば, たら, なら
chapters.description: Express conditions and hypotheticals using four different forms.

lessons.prompt: Which conditional is used for natural/automatic consequences?
lessons.explanation: 'To' = automatic/natural result. 'Kono michi o iku to, eki ga arimasu' = If you take this road, there's a station.

lessons.prompt: もっと勉強すれ___、合格できる。(If you study more, you can pass.)
lessons.explanation: 'Ba' conditional: replace final u with eba (godan) or add reba (ichidan). Expresses general condition.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'If it rains, I won't go'
lessons.correctAnswer: 雨が降ったら行きません
lessons.hint: 'Tara' conditional: past form + ra. 降った (rained) + ら = if it rains. Focuses on that specific event.

lessons.prompt: When is 〜なら (nara) used?
options: Advice based on what you've heard
lessons.correctAnswer: Advice based on what you've heard
lessons.explanation: 'Nara' reacts to what was just said. 'Hiroshima ni iku nara, okonomiyaki o tabete' = If you're going to Hiroshima, eat okonomiyaki.

lessons.prompt: 東京に行っ___、スカイツリーを見てください。(If you go to Tokyo, please see the Skytree.)
lessons.explanation: 'Ittara' = if you go (upon going). Tara = past stem + ra. Good for specific situation advice.
